type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Adding resolve_image_with_size(), resolve_audio_with_size(), resolve_document_with_size() methods to ImageBlock, AudioBlock, DocumentBlock classes respectively (new methods, placed after existing resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document methods)

type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I will modify the resolve_image(), resolve_audio(), and resolve_document() methods on ImageBlock/AudioBlock/DocumentBlock to raise ValueError when the resolved BytesIO buffer has zero bytes.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document methods
code:     def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.image,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_image returned zero bytes")
        return buffer
note: Since your new resolve_image_with_size (and audio/document equivalents) will be inserted immediately after these existing methods, our edits are physically adjacent and could conflict. Proposing this body for resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document (adding zero-byte validation before return). Please send back the exact verbatim text of your resolve_image_with_size/resolve_audio_with_size/resolve_document_with_size methods so we can agree on the full merged block (existing method + your new method back to back) for each of the 3 classes.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: ImageBlock.resolve_image + resolve_image_with_size
code: def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
    """
    Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

    Args:
        as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

    """
    buffer = resolve_binary(
        raw_bytes=self.image,
        path=self.path,
        url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
        as_base64=as_base64,
    )
    if not buffer.getbuffer().nbytes:
        raise ValueError("Resolved image is empty")
    return buffer

def resolve_image_with_size(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """
    Resolve an image such that PIL can read it, and also return its byte size.

    Args:
        as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

    Returns:
        tuple[BytesIO, int]: the resolved image buffer and its size in bytes

    """
    buffer = self.resolve_image(as_base64=as_base64)
    size = buffer.getbuffer().nbytes
    return buffer, size
note: Proposing exact check buffer.getbuffer().nbytes and error message; let me know if you planned something different (e.g. len(buffer.getvalue())) so we converge on identical text. Same pattern applies to resolve_audio/resolve_audio_with_size and resolve_document/resolve_document_with_size.

note: My task spec explicitly requires: seek(0,2)/tell()/seek(0) to measure size, and message must be exactly 'resolve_<type> returned zero bytes' (e.g. 'resolve_image returned zero bytes'), not 'Resolved image is empty' or getbuffer().nbytes. Kept your resolve_image_with_size but reused the same seek/tell measurement so both features share one mechanism. Please confirm or send back if this breaks your with_size logic.

code:     def resolve_document(self) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve a document such that it is represented by a BufferIO object.
        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.data,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=False,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_document returned zero bytes")
        return buffer

    def resolve_document_with_size(self) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
        """
        Resolve a document such that it is represented by a BufferIO object, and also return its byte size.

        Returns:
            tuple[BytesIO, int]: the resolved document buffer and its size in bytes

        """
        buffer = self.resolve_document()
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        return buffer, size
note: Same pattern applied to DocumentBlock (no as_base64 param, matches existing resolve_document signature). Please confirm.
